mechanism_narrative: Persistent low unemployment indicates a strong labor market, supporting consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns. This strength suggests a robust economic foundation.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative or flat on average when UNRATE is below 4.0% over a 3-year rolling period, or if the correlation is not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in retail sales indicates weakening consumer demand, signaling potential economic contraction and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as corporate earnings expectations fall due to reduced consumption.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances where RSAFS 3-month annualized growth falls below -5% over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation combined with rising interest rates signals a restrictive monetary policy environment that dampens economic activity and increases the cost of capital for businesses, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 30% of periods where both CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3% and FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate during an economic downturn signals a deteriorating labor market, which will further depress consumer confidence and spending, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.7% (t-1mo) is currently low, but a sharp rise would be significant
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive on average when UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points in 3 months during a declared recession.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production indicates robust manufacturing and supply-side activity, which supports corporate earnings and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns as economic expansion continues.
failure_condition: If the correlation between INDPRO growth and S&P 500 5-day forward returns is negative or statistically insignificant over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant drop in the federal funds rate signals aggressive monetary easing, which aims to stimulate economic activity by lowering borrowing costs and typically leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.06% (t-1mo) is high, suggesting potential for cuts
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative on average when FEDFUNDS drops by more than 0.5 percentage points in 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of declining housing starts and falling payrolls indicates a broad-based contraction in both investment and labor demand, signaling a severe economic downturn and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month annualized growth < -10% AND PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth < -1%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1401.0 (t-2mo) and PAYEMS at 156105.0 (t-1mo) are showing signs of weakness in the current environment (2008-12-15 context)
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 25% of periods where both housing starts and payrolls show significant contraction.

mechanism_narrative: A significant contraction in GDP growth signals a broad economic slowdown or recession, which directly impacts corporate revenues and profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as investor sentiment deteriorates.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth falls below 0%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 20246.439 (t-5mo) is showing signs of weakness in the current environment (2008-12-15 context)
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive on average when GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth is negative over a 10-year period.
